keyTerms.Globe Theatre: Shakespeare's open-air playhouse on the south bank of the Thames, where groundlings paid a penny to stand and the wealthy sat in galleries
keyTerms.groundlings: The standing audience in the pit of Elizabethan theaters — rowdy, interactive, and socially diverse, eating and heckling through performances
keyTerms.Elizabeth I: The Virgin Queen who ruled England from 1558 to 1603, overseeing the flourishing of English drama during Shakespeare's career
keyTerms.Hamnet: Shakespeare's only son, who died at age eleven in 1596; his name echoes through the play Hamlet, written a few years later
keyTerms.bear-baiting: A popular Elizabethan blood sport in which chained bears were attacked by dogs, competing with theater for London's entertainment penny
